translator.name: The Koran (Alcoran of Mohammed)
translator.attribution: George Sale, The Koran, Commonly Called the Alcoran of Mohammed (London, 1734).
translator.note: SAMPLE renderings of Sale's archaic English (included to demonstrate interpretive divergence and as an unambiguously public-domain third translation). Verify against a scanned source before shipping.

verses.1:1: In the name of the most merciful God.
verses.1:2: Praise be to God, the Lord of all creatures;
verses.1:4: the King of the day of judgment.
verses.1:5: Thee do we worship, and of thee do we beg assistance.
verses.1:6: Direct us in the right way,
verses.1:7: in the way of those to whom thou hast been gracious; not of those against whom thou art incensed, nor of those who go astray.
